question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with limit=0 (triggering hasLimit()=false) and a very old startTime to force the service to stream an unbounded number of historical messages, bypassing the per-subscription resource cap and inflating backend processing costs outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user open thousands of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via subscribeTopic() to drive subscriberCount to an arbitrarily large value, exhausting server thread/connection resources and causing fee-related processing to degrade for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to the minimum allowed value (0 nanoseconds) and no endTime, causing subscribeTopic() to initiate an unbounded historical replay that consumes database I/O and compute resources far beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly subscribe and immediately cancel via subscribeTopic() to cause subscriberCount.incrementAndGet() and decrementAndGet() to race, producing metric noise that masks real subscriber load and distorts fee-related capacity planning?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the checkTopicExists=false configuration path in topicExists() to subscribe to a non-existent topic ID, causing the service to issue repeated database queries for a phantom entity and consuming resources without any valid fee-bearing transaction backing the topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user supply a topicId that resolves to a non-TOPIC entity type, causing topicExists() to emit an IllegalArgumentException after the entityRepository.findById() call, and repeat this pattern at high frequency to generate excessive database lookups and error-handling overhead?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user enumerate large ranges of EntityId values in topicId to probe topicExists() and cause cache thrashing in the entityCacheSize=50000 cache, evicting legitimate entries and increasing database query rates for other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the safety-check path in missingMessages() (current==null) by holding a subscription open without receiving any live messages, causing the service to issue a gap-recovery retrieve() call every second indefinitely, multiplying database query costs beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user craft a scenario where numMissingMessages = current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1 evaluates to a very large positive number, causing missingMessages() to issue a topicMessageRetriever.retrieve() call with an enormous limit, consuming excessive database and network resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a high-volume topic where sequence gaps are common, causing missingMessages() to be invoked on every incoming message and triggering a cascade of retrieve() calls that amplify resource consumption far beyond the single-subscription design budget?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user set limit=1 in TopicMessageFilter and then rapidly re-subscribe after each message, causing incomingMessages() to repeatedly construct new filter objects via filter.toBuilder().limit(limit).startTime(startTime).build() and re-register with topicListener, generating listener registration overhead beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the limit recalculation in incomingMessages() — limit = filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get() — by subscribing with a very large limit value (Long.MAX_VALUE) to cause the computed remaining limit to overflow or produce unexpected behavior in the downstream retriever?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic with startTime set to a value just before a burst of millions of historical messages, causing incomingMessages() to compute a startTime from last.getConsensusTimestamp()+1 that still covers the entire burst, triggering a massive retrieve() call?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a value just slightly in the future in TopicMessageFilter, causing pastEndTime() to poll with grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval() (30 seconds) and keep the subscription alive far longer than the actual data window, consuming server resources beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to Long.MAX_VALUE in TopicMessageFilter, causing pastEndTime() to return Flux.never() (since endTime is not null but isComplete() never returns true), holding the subscription open indefinitely and consuming resources without bound?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the RepeatSpec.create() loop in pastEndTime() — which repeats up to Long.MAX_VALUE times — by setting endTime just beyond the current time, causing the repeat loop to spin at the endTimeInterval rate and generate excessive scheduler activity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user craft a filter where endTime is set to a value that makes isComplete() return false indefinitely (e.g., endTime far in the future), preventing the subscription from terminating and causing the server to maintain state and resources for the subscription beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a nanosecond timestamp just after startTime in isComplete(), causing the Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, filter.getEndTime()).plus(grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval()) check to keep the subscription alive for an extra 30 seconds beyond the intended window, inflating resource usage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the condition filter.getEndTime() < startTime in isComplete() by setting endTime to exactly equal startTime (not less than), causing isComplete() to return false and keeping the subscription alive when it should immediately terminate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the IllegalStateException in onNext() by subscribing to a topic where out-of-order messages are delivered, causing the service to throw and log an exception that reveals internal sequence state (last and current TopicMessage details) to the attacker via error responses?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.rate()] Can an unprivileged user cause the rate() calculation — (int)(1000.0 * count.get() / elapsed) — to overflow by holding a subscription open long enough for count to reach Integer.MAX_VALUE, producing a negative or zero rate that corrupts the finished() log entry and misleads operators about actual resource consumption?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.finished()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic and then cancel immediately, causing finished() to log a message with subscriberId (a random 8-char string) that could be crafted to inject log-format characters (e.g., newlines, format specifiers) into the log output, corrupting audit trails used for fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set limit to Long.MAX_VALUE in TopicMessageFilter, causing flux.take(filter.getLimit()) to request Long.MAX_VALUE elements from the upstream publisher, effectively removing the limit and consuming unbounded resources in violation of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) in subscribeTopic() by subscribing to a topic with no live messages, causing the safetyCheck Flux to fire after 1 second and trigger missingMessages() with a null current, which then calls retrieve() — and repeat this pattern indefinitely to generate continuous database load?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic with endTime=null and limit=0, causing subscribeTopic() to create a subscription that never terminates (pastEndTime returns Flux.never(), no limit applied), holding server resources indefinitely and consuming compute/memory beyond design parameters?
